74191.subject: OpenAI is killing ChatGPT as you know it
74191.body:
----------
######                      [Sign Up](http://utm.genai.works/r/gg2ubr5) | [Collaborate with us](https://6hhfxazcbpc.typeform.com/to/uXIl0IFH?utm_source=atlas&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=general_2026_05_11&utm_content=banner_post)


--------------------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8776fa6e-e2d0-4aec-9648-1bed6de3a659/GenAI_Daily_Banner.jpg?t=1778687445)
Caption: 


----------

----------
**Welcome back!** OpenAI is rebuilding ChatGPT into an app that runs your day, and it's burying the plain chatbot to do it. Nvidia handed a Korean industrial giant the brains for its robots. And the cost of capable AI showed up twice: Microsoft found seven ways to hijack an agent, and the US is scrambling to keep its best hacking AI away from China.

**In today's Generative AI Newsletter:**

* **OpenAI's everything-app:** Why is OpenAI burying the chatbot that made it famous?

* **Nvidia's AI robots:** How does a factory robot learn to sand a panel with nobody coding the moves?

* **Microsoft's 7 agent hacks:** How do you hijack an AI agent with one plain-English sentence?

* **America's hacking AI:** What happens when an AI finds security holes faster than anyone can patch them?


----------

----------
## [OpenAI is killing ChatGPT as you know it](https://www.ft.com/content/ca0f5f5e-fb9a-41a0-a2a9-0127e15b7db9?syn-25a6b1a6=1)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/07d5d265-c23d-40fe-87ee-da2f90c7c540/Beehiiv_01_-_Jun_8.png?t=1780910950)
Caption: 

OpenAI is rebuilding ChatGPT into a superapp that does your tasks and runs other companies' software to finish them. The Financial Times reports a redesign in the coming weeks with AI agents handling multi-step jobs and partner apps living right inside it. The model is WeChat, the Chinese app that folded messaging, payments and shopping into one screen nobody ever leaves.

The reason is money, and the timing gives it away. ChatGPT has 900 million weekly users but only 50 million pay, and its 2 million business customers already drive 40% of revenue. With a public listing weeks out, that enterprise money is the number OpenAI wants in front of investors. It's serious enough to have sidelined Sora, the video app it launched last year.

An agent that acts for you can turn a small mistake into an expensive one, and OpenAI is betting hundreds of millions of people will let it try. Microsoft promised the same kind of Copilot superapp for this summer and hasn't shipped it, so the first one out the door sets the standard.


----------



----------
## [**How to make AI agents actually work at scale**](https://link.genai.works/FzZK)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/aa1e26f5-3eaf-4322-afa

74190.subject: The Top 100 AI & Tech Investors of 2026 💸
74190.body:
View this post on the web at https://linas.substack.com/p/top-100-ai-tech-vc-investors-2026

👋 Hey, Linas here! Every day, I break down 3 stories shaping the future of FinTech & Artificial Intelligence - plus the money movements and trends worth tracking. First time here? 389k+ FinTech and AI leaders get this daily. Join them:
A check from these people doesn’t just fund a startup. It tells every other investor in the room to pay attention.
They were the early money behind Anthropic [ https://substack.com/redirect/66b251d2-d26b-431f-9540-504dff352f01?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ], OpenAI [ https://substack.com/redirect/5f6b899f-0e6f-4e41-b769-843dcf421e3a?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ], SpaceX [ https://substack.com/redirect/605ba48e-0190-49bd-995b-3611684b1a4c?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ], Stripe [ https://substack.com/redirect/6749af5f-5d1b-4813-a6cc-ca78c0f091a1?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ], and Revolut [ https://substack.com/redirect/d52e3119-1a82-409d-bcba-b79a4d46a566?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] - often years before the rest of the world caught on. Vinod Khosla wrote a $50 million check to OpenAI [ https://substack.com/redirect/62467dd2-10b5-4d36-9418-ffee7715d32f?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] in 2019, when the company was still a nonprofit research lab. That stake alone is now worth an estimated $8 billion.
He’s not the only one on this list who saw the future early: eight of these investors backed OpenAI, eleven backed Stripe, eight backed SpaceX, and nine backed Databricks - in most cases well before the consensus formed.
So who are the 100 investors with that kind of power in 2026, and how do you actually get in front of them?
We started with the Forbes Midas List, the established benchmark for ranking the world’s top venture capitalists. Then we went further: broader coverage, deeper profiles, their most recent bets, and the one thing Forbes never hands you - direct, verified contact details.
Every profile is current, fact-checked, and built for action. You’ll find each investor’s firm, location, and the bets that built their reputation - from Gili Raanan’s early conviction in Wiz to Neil Shen’s prescient backing of ByteDance. You’ll see what they’re deploying capital into right now, so you can match your raise to the right partner. And you’ll get verified LinkedIn and X handles so you can reach out directly instead of waiting for the warm intro that may never come.
Inside, you’ll find six Sequoia Capital partners, the Founders Fund duo behind SpaceX and Anduril, three partners each from Andreessen Horowitz and Accel, the Index Ventures team that backed Revolut and Figma from London, and the DST Global and Coatue [ https://substack.com/redirect/537c1471-4d37-4509-a660-78425c529c57?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In

74187.subject: OpenGradient Launches Privacy-First Generative AI Platform
74187.body:
# 

People now bring their most consequential questions to AI: a worrying symptom, a tax decision, a legal bind, the thing they would never say out loud. But the assistants they ask were built to remember. Messages get tied to an account, logged, and made eligible to train the next model. It is a quiet bargain users never agreed to: get help, but hand over the most sensitive details of your life to do it.



View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/069f660c-e57e-4b1f-bbdc-fe8ed5490833/OpenGradient_Chat_is_a_privacy_focused_AI_app_that_routes_to_frontier.jpg?t=1780766947)
Caption: ==OpenGradient Chat is a privacy-focused AI app that routes to frontier text, image, and video models, through an anonymizing layer (local encryption, Oblivious HTTP, and secure enclaves) so your prompts are never linked to your identity.==

Today, OpenGradient launched OpenGradient Chat, a generative AI assistant built on the opposite premise: the questions that matter most are exactly the ones you should be able to ask without attaching your name to them. Finally, an AI you can tell anything.





———————————————————————————



## **Privacy you can verify**

Most assistants ask you to trust a privacy policy, a written promise to handle your data well. OpenGradient Chat replaces the promise with proof.

Privacy is enforced in the architecture, in three layers:

* **Encrypted on your device.** Messages are encrypted locally before they leave the browser, with keys that live only on your device.

* **Routed through an Oblivious HTTP relay.** The relay sees your IP but only ciphertext; the downstream gateway sees plaintext but never your IP. No single party can correlate identity with content.

* **Processed inside a TEE-isolated gateway.** Prompts are decrypted only inside a trusted execution environment with remote attestation. Memory is sealed, and the operator can't read or log it.

==Because the enclave is attested, you can verify these guarantees yourself rather than take OpenGradient's word for them. OpenGradient never holds a message it could trace back to you. It is the same verifiable foundation as OpenGradient's core network, now in an everyday assistant.==

Tweet:  (https://twitter.com/OpenGradient/status/2062630158188658713?s=20)

#### **Every frontier model, in one app**

==Underneath the privacy, it is a full assistant. A single app reaches the frontier models (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok, and ByteDance Seed, to begin with) and lets you switch mid-conversation or run two side by side. It also includes live web search, uncensored image generation, and file uploads for PDFs, documents, code, and images. Anyone who signs up receives 1,000 free credits.==



#### **What's next**

OpenGradient Chat starts with text and images, and the roadmap is broader. Dedicated image and video models are coming to the same workspace, with the same
